channel.title: AI English Daily
channel.description: Daily AI news delivered as business English presentations. Curated for data scientists and engineering managers. Practice shadowing, expand vocabulary, master presentation frameworks.
channel.itunes:author: Kenichiro Nishioka
channel.itunes:summary: Daily AI news delivered as business English presentations.

item.title: AI English Daily — 06/12
item.description:
Today's framework: Situation→Complication→Resolution — Frame the current context, name the tension or risk, then close with the practical resolution.
📰 Stories covered: • AWS ML Blog: Agent-EvalKit — process-level evals for tool-using agents. • xAI News: Grok Build Plugin Marketplace — installable skills, agents, hooks, MCP servers, and LSPs. • Anthropic News: DXC alliance — Claude moves deeper into regulated enterprise systems. • GitHub Changelog: Copilot agent sessions — chat can inspect logs and session history. • Anthropic News: Claude Corps — fellowship model for AI capacity in nonprofits.
✨ Featured expressions: sets the stage for, as critical as, moving beyond (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: agentic systems /eɪˈdʒen.tɪk ˈsɪs.təmz/, tool calls /tuːl kɔːlz/, intermediate state /ˌɪn.tərˈmiː.di.ət steɪt/
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 06/11
item.description:
Today's framework: What→So What→Now What — A concise structure for explaining what happened, why it matters, and what action should come next.
📰 Stories covered: • Anthropic: Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5 — Anthropic launched Claude Fable 5 for general use and Mythos 5 for trusted cyberdefense partners. • GitHub Changelog: Security validation for third-party coding agents — GitHub made automated security validation generally available for third-party coding agents. • OpenAI: From data to decisions: how LSEG is scaling trusted AI — OpenAI showed how LSEG cut AI-enabled product cycles from months to about two weeks. • OpenAI: How engineers at Nextdoor use Codex to build without limits — OpenAI described how Nextdoor uses Codex to shift engineers toward outcome engineering. • xAI: Powering Gopuff's Go agent — xAI said Gopuff launched Go, a multimodal shopping assistant powered by Grok models.
✨ Featured expressions: raise the bar, an essential component of the trust boundary, the bottlenecks are moving upstream (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: agentic development //eɪˈdʒen.tɪk dɪˈvel.əp.mənt//, CodeQL //koʊd kjuː el//, outcome engineering //ˈaʊt.kʌm ˌen.dʒɪˈnɪr.ɪŋ//
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 06/10
item.description:
Today's framework: Before → After → Bridge — Contrast the old state, the new state, and the practical bridge teams need to cross.
📰 Stories covered: • Anthropic News: Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5 — Anthropic launched Claude Fable 5 for general use and Claude Mythos 5 for a trusted-access cyberdefense and infrastructure program. • AWS Machine Learning Blog: It’s safe to close your laptop now: Hosting coding agents on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore — AWS described how Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime can host coding agents in isolated Linux microVMs with persistent workspaces, shell access, deterministic command execution, identity, and gateway-managed tools. • OpenAI News: Confidential submission of draft S-1 to the SEC — OpenAI announced it had confidentially submitted a draft S-1 and said timing for any public listing has not been decided. • xAI News: Powering Gopuff's Go agent — xAI announced a collaboration with Gopuff to launch Go, an AI-powered shopping assistant in the Gopuff app that uses Grok text, audio, and image models.
✨ Featured expressions: at the helm, tethered to individual developer laptops, operating outside the full glare of public market scrutiny (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: microVMs /ˈmaɪkroʊ ˌviː ˈɛmz/, persistent workspaces /pərˈsɪstənt ˈwɜːrkˌspeɪsɪz/, gateway-managed tools /ˈɡeɪtweɪ ˈmænɪdʒd tuːlz/
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 06/09
item.description:
Today's framework: Problem → Solution → Impact — Frame the business problem, show the concrete solution, then spell out the operational impact.
📰 Stories covered: • AWS News Blog: Try the new console experience in Amazon Bedrock, optimized for Anthropic- and OpenAI-compatible APIs — AWS announced a new Amazon Bedrock console experience for the bedrock-mantle endpoint. • GitHub Changelog: Expanded technical preview availability for the GitHub Copilot app — GitHub expanded the Copilot app technical preview to existing paid Copilot customers and introduced canvases. • GitHub Changelog: Agent tasks REST API now available for Copilot Pro, Pro+, and Max — GitHub made the Agent tasks REST API available in public preview for Copilot Pro. • GitHub Changelog: Larger context windows and configurable reasoning levels for GitHub Copilot — GitHub Copilot added support for one-million-token context windows and configurable reasoning levels in VS Code. • Vercel Changelog: Updates to Legal Terms — Vercel updated its Terms of Service and Marketplace terms to define AI Functionality and Third-Party Tools.
✨ Featured expressions: To put that in context, Put differently, The practical takeaway is (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: agentic software development /eɪˈdʒen.tɪk ˈsɔːft.wer dɪˈvel.əp.mənt/, context window /ˈkɑːn.tekst ˈwɪn.doʊ/, reasoning levels /ˈriː.zən.ɪŋ ˈlev.əlz/
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 06/08
item.description:
Today's framework: PREP — Point → Reason → Example → Point: state the message, justify it, make it concrete, then restate the takeaway.
📰 Stories covered: • Hugging Face Blog: Her · हेर — a detective for your Claude Code sessions — A Hugging Face community project, Her, analyzes Claude Code session JSONL traces, reconstructs risky actions, token use, tools, subagents, skills, and MCP servers, and keeps findings deterministic while using a small local model only for prose. • Latent Space: [AINews] not much happened today — Latent Space summarized a quiet AI news day where agent benchmarks, agent runtimes, agent-optimized tooling, inference cost controls, and Lockdown Mode-style security features stood out more than new frontier model releases.
✨ Featured expressions: shifting the paradigm, no longer a luxury but a fundamental requirement, the market is unequivocally signaling (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: agent observability /ˈeɪdʒənt əbˌzɝːvəˈbɪləti/, deterministic forensic analysis /dɪˌtɝːmɪˈnɪstɪk fəˈrɛnsɪk əˈnæləsɪs/, JSONL /ˌdʒeɪsənˈɛl/
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 06/07
item.description:
Today's framework: What→So What→Now What — Move from the facts, to their business meaning, to the practical next actions.
📰 Stories covered: • Anthropic Institute: When AI builds itself — Anthropic says AI is already speeding up AI development, with Claude authoring much of its merged code. • Simon Willison: Running Python code in a sandbox with MicroPython and WASM — Simon Willison covers practical agent security: sandboxed Python execution and Lockdown Mode against data exfiltration. • The Batch: Alibaba's Latest Proprietary Model, Qwen3.7-Max, Challenges U.S. Rivals — The Batch reports Qwen3.7-Max pushing long-context, agentic model competition forward. • AWS Machine Learning Blog: How to build self-driving AI operations on Amazon Bedrock at scale — AWS introduces Bedrock Ops Alert for production monitoring and support automation.
✨ Featured expressions: underscoring the rapid evolution, reframes how we should view, no longer just assistance tools (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: recursive self-improvement /rɪˈkɝːsɪv self ˌɪmpruːvˈmənt/, sandbox /ˈsændˌbɑːks/, data exfiltration /ˈdeɪtə eksˌfɪltreɪʃən/
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 06/06
item.description:
Today's framework: Situation→Complication→Resolution — Frame the current state, identify the tension, then present the practical resolution.
📰 Stories covered: • Cursor Changelog: Custom stores, custom tools, and auto-review for the Cursor SDK — Cursor shipped SDK features for custom tools, JSONL or custom state stores, auto-review routing for local tool calls, and nested subagents. • OpenAI: Biodefense in the Intelligence Age — OpenAI launched a biodefense action plan tied to advanced biology capabilities and Rosalind Biodefense, focused on detection, countermeasure development, and crisis response.
✨ Featured expressions: delegated intelligence, a proactive and comprehensive approach, production-grade, programmable infrastructure (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: agent infrastructure //ˈeɪdʒənt ˈɪnfrəˌstrʌktʃər//, state store //steɪt stɔːr//, auto-review routing //ˈɔːtoʊ rɪˈvjuː ˈruːtɪŋ//
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 06/05
item.description:
Today's framework: Situation→Complication→Resolution — Frame the context, expose the tension, then close with practical next steps.
📰 Stories covered: • OpenAI: Dreaming: Better memory for a more helpful ChatGPT — OpenAI launched a more capable and compute-efficient ChatGPT memory architecture built on its background 'dreaming' process, with reviewable memory summaries for users. • GitHub Changelog: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio Code, May releases — GitHub detailed VS Code Copilot updates including the Agents window in Stable preview, remote agent sessions, session sync, BYOK expansion, and command risk assessment. • xAI: Grok Imagine 1.5 Preview — xAI made grok-imagine-video-1. • OpenAI: A blueprint for democratic governance of frontier AI — OpenAI released a policy blueprint for a durable U. • xAI: Grok Becomes the Voice of Vapi — xAI partnered with Vapi so Grok powers Vapi's 12 core voices and becomes available for speech-to-text and text-to-speech in the Vapi dashboard.
✨ Featured expressions: a user-facing product surface, a hidden personalization trick, the interface has to support supervision (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: memory architecture /ˈmeməri ˈɑːrkɪtektʃər/, bring-your-own-key /brɪŋ jʊər oʊn kiː/, Agent Host Protocol /ˈeɪdʒənt hoʊst ˈproʊtəkɔːl/
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 06/04
item.description:
Today's framework: What→So What→Now What — Use What to state the update, So What to explain business impact, and Now What to turn it into practical action.
📰 Stories covered: • Google Developers Blog: Gemma 4 12B on laptops — Google showed local agentic workflows with AI Edge Gallery, Eloquent, and LiteRT-LM. • Hugging Face / Ideogram: Ideogram 4 — Ideogram released weights and inference code for a controllable 9.3B open image model. • Anthropic: Claude Partner Hub — Anthropic introduced a tiered partner program after 40,000+ firm applications and 10,000+ certifications. • OpenAI Developers: API deprecations and billing — OpenAI deprecated prompt objects, Evals, and Agent Builder, and changed container billing.
✨ Featured expressions: bypassing the need for cloud infrastructure, formalizing the support and expertise required, critical gating factors (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: edge execution //edʒ ˌeksɪˈkjuːʃən//, open-weight model //ˌoʊpən weɪt ˈmɑːdəl//, structured JSON prompting //ˈstrʌktʃərd ˈdʒeɪsən ˈprɑːmptɪŋ//
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 06/03
item.description:
Today's framework: Before→After→Bridge — Contrast the old way of working with the new reality, then connect the two with a practical transition.
📰 Stories covered: • OpenAI: Codex is becoming a productivity tool for everyone — OpenAI released a report saying Codex now has more than 5 million weekly active users and is increasingly used by knowledge workers for reports, spreadsheets, research, data analysis, workflow automation, and lightweight tools. • GitHub Blog: Updates to GitHub Copilot billing and plans — GitHub said usage-based billing is now live for all Copilot plans, with consumption based on GitHub AI Credits; Copilot code review also consumes GitHub Actions minutes, and user-level budgets are generally available. • Microsoft News: Mayo Clinic and Microsoft collaborate to develop a frontier AI model for healthcare — Mayo Clinic and Microsoft announced a collaboration to build and deploy a healthcare-specific frontier AI model using Mayo's de-identified clinical data and Microsoft's AI, cloud, engineering, and superintelligence capabilities. • xAI: Grok Composer 2.5 — xAI launched Grok Composer 2. • Simon Willison: Hackers Simply Asked Meta AI to Give Them Access to High-Profile Instagram Accounts. It Worked — Simon Willison highlighted reporting that attackers manipulated Meta's AI support bot into linking Instagram accounts to attacker-controlled email addresses during account recovery.
✨ Featured expressions: grappling with the economic and security implications, a new layer of financial accountability, from the outset (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: agentic coding workflows /eɪˈdʒen.tɪk ˈkoʊ.dɪŋ ˈwɝːk.floʊz/, inference cost /ˈɪn.fɚ.əns kɔːst/, function calling /ˈfʌŋk.ʃən ˈkɔː.lɪŋ/
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 06/02
item.description:
Today's framework: Problem → Solution → Impact — Frame the business problem, explain the practical response, then make the impact explicit.
📰 Stories covered: • Cursor Changelog: Auto-review Run Mode — Cursor 3. • Mistral AI: Introducing Search Toolkit — Mistral released Search Toolkit in public preview, an open-source framework that unifies ingestion, retrieval, and evaluation for production search pipelines in AI applications. • AWS Machine Learning Blog: Evaluating Deep Agents using LangSmith on AWS — AWS published a practical guide for evaluating deep agents with LangSmith on AWS, covering offline evaluations, online monitoring, and patterns such as trajectory grading, final-response grading, and state inspection. • Vercel Changelog: Opus 4.8 on AI Gateway — Vercel added Claude Opus 4. • Modal Labs Blog: Role-Based Access Control for humans and agents — Modal made Role-Based Access Control available to Team and Enterprise users, with environments and restricted environments designed to separate human, CI, external caller, and AI-agent permissions.
✨ Featured expressions: policy-gated autonomy, retrieval quality, the failure chain (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: sandbox //ˈsænd.bɑːks//, classifier subagent //ˈklæs.ə.faɪ.ər ˈsʌbˌeɪ.dʒənt//, ingestion //ɪnˈdʒes.tʃən//
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 06/01
item.description:
Today's framework: PREP — Point -> Reason -> Example -> Point: state the conclusion, explain why, support it with evidence, then restate the takeaway.
📰 Stories covered: • Anthropic Engineering via Simon Willison: How we contain Claude across products — Anthropic documented how Claude. • xAI News: Introducing Grok Build — xAI introduced Grok Build, an early-beta terminal coding agent for SuperGrok and X Premium Plus subscribers. • OpenAI Developers: Codex pricing and usage promo ends May 31 — OpenAI’s Codex pricing page says the Pro $100 tier’s 2x Codex usage promotion runs through May 31, 2026, while Pro $200 carries forward 20x Plus usage on an ongoing basis. • xAI News: Connectors in web, iOS, and Android — xAI launched Grok Connectors for web, iOS, and Android, bringing external apps into Grok so it can read, summarize, update, and organize everyday work across tools.
✨ Featured expressions: The practical takeaway is, Put differently, To put that in context (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: sandbox /ˈsændbɑːks/, egress control /ˈiːɡres kənˈtroʊl/, connector /kəˈnektər/
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 05/31
item.description:
Today's framework: Situation → Complication → Resolution — Set context, name the tension, then close with a practical path forward.
📰 Stories covered: • OpenAI Help Center: Codex updates: Computer use and remote control for Windows, usage profiles — OpenAI added Windows Computer Use support to the Codex app, remote steering from ChatGPT mobile or Codex on Mac, infrastructure improvements, and Codex Profiles for usage and activity visibility. • xAI: Grok Build 0.1 on API — xAI made grok-build-0. • Vercel Blog: Protecting against inference theft — Vercel described inference theft as unauthorized resale of paid AI inference and showed why AI endpoints need per-request verification rather than session-only checks. • AWS Machine Learning Blog: Comprehensive observability for Amazon SageMaker AI LLM inference: From GPU utilization to LLM quality — AWS published a solution that combines SageMaker AI inference components, CloudWatch metrics, and Amazon Managed Grafana dashboards to monitor LLM infrastructure and output quality together.
✨ Featured expressions: the common thread in today's stories is, becoming less like chat windows and more like remote operators, the asset being stolen is no longer just data or access (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: inference theft /ˈɪnfərəns θeft/, agentic coding /eɪˈdʒentɪk ˈkoʊdɪŋ/, observability /əbˌzɝːvəˈbɪləti/
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 05/30
item.description:
Today's framework: Problem -> Solution -> Impact — Frame the core challenge, show the operating response, then explain the business implications.
📰 Stories covered: • OpenAI Blog: Strengthening societal resilience with Rosalind Biodefense — OpenAI launched Rosalind Biodefense and expanded trusted access to GPT-Rosalind for vetted public-health, government, and allied biodefense partners. • OpenAI Blog: A shared playbook for trustworthy third party evaluations — OpenAI published guidance for third-party frontier-model evaluations, emphasizing explicit claims, harness design, validity evidence, and hazards like reward hacking, contamination, refusals, broken problems, and sandbagging. • OpenAI Codex Changelog: Computer use and mobile access on Windows 26.527 — OpenAI extended Codex Computer Use to Windows and added Windows support for remote control, letting Codex operate Windows desktop apps and be started or monitored from other devices. • OpenAI Blog: OpenAI’s Frontier Governance Framework — OpenAI published a Frontier Governance Framework aligning its safety and security practices with emerging legal requirements such as California’s Transparency in Frontier AI Act and the EU AI Act’s Code of Practice.
✨ Featured expressions: governed capability, controlled access, supervised delegation (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: harness //ˈhɑːr.nəs//, biodefense //ˌbaɪ.oʊ.dɪˈfens//, non-pharmaceutical interventions //ˌnɑːn ˌfɑːr.məˈsuː.tɪ.kəl ˌɪn.tərˈven.ʃənz//
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 05/29
item.description:
Today's framework: Situation → Complication → Resolution — Frame the current context, name the tension, then close with a practical resolution.
📰 Stories covered: • Anthropic Official Blog: Anthropic raises $65B in Series H funding at $965B post-money valuation — Anthropic announced a $65 billion Series H round at a $965 billion post-money valuation, backed by major investors and infrastructure partners. • Mistral AI News: AI Now Summit 2026 — Mistral announced an industrial engineering stack, a unified Vibe agent for long-horizon work, and a dedicated 10 MW inference data center in Les Ulis. • Mistral AI News: Introducing Search Toolkit — Mistral released Search Toolkit in public preview as an open-source framework covering ingestion, retrieval, and evaluation for production search pipelines. • xAI News: Grok Build 0.1 on API — xAI made grok-build-0. • Mistral AI Customers: Mission-focused AI: How HTX transforms Singapore’s public safety with custom and local AI — Mistral described how Singapore’s HTX built Phoenix Small and Medium models on NGINE using Mistral Forge for controlled, public-safety-focused AI deployment.
✨ Featured expressions: underscores a critical shift, the overall picture reveals, beyond just acquiring powerful models (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: post-money valuation //poʊst ˈmʌni ˌvæljuˈeɪʃən//, inference data center //ˈɪnfərəns ˈdeɪtə ˈsentər//, retrieval quality //rɪˈtriːvəl ˈkwɑːləti//
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 05/28
item.description:
Today's framework: What→So What→Now What — Clarify what happened, why it matters, and what to do next.
📰 Stories covered: • OpenAI: OpenAI turns tax-production traces into Codex eval loops. • AWS Machine Learning Blog: AWS frames payments and budget guardrails as agent infrastructure. • LangChain Blog: Lyft shows self-serve support-agent iteration with tracing and evals. • Hugging Face Blog: ITBench-AA shows frontier models below 50% on enterprise SRE tasks. • Mistral AI News: Mistral buys Emmi AI to push physics-aware industrial agents.
✨ Featured expressions: moving from impressive demos into operating systems for real work, money movement as a first-class engineering concern, every failure can become a hill to climb (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: production traces //prəˈdʌkʃən treɪsɪz//, payment rails //ˈpeɪmənt reɪlz//, observability //əbˌzɝːvəˈbɪləti//
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 05/27
item.description:
Today's framework: Before → After → Bridge — Show the old reality, the new reality, and the practical path between them.
📰 Stories covered: • SemiAnalysis InferenceX: AMD MI355X Qwen3.5 397B-A17B Inference: Up to 19x Throughput per GPU in 3 Months on SGLang FP8 — SemiAnalysis reported that AMD MI355X SGLang FP8 throughput on Qwen3. • Mistral AI: Introducing Mistral 3 — Mistral introduced Mistral 3, including Mistral Large 3, a 675B-parameter sparse mixture-of-experts model with 41B active parameters, plus small Ministral models for edge and local use. • Anthropic: Project Glasswing: An initial update — Anthropic said Project Glasswing partners have found more than ten thousand high- or critical-severity vulnerabilities with Claude Mythos Preview, and that early open-source scanning surfaced thousands of likely serious findings.
✨ Featured expressions: To put that in context, Another way to frame it is, The practical takeaway is (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: inference /ˈɪnfərəns/, throughput /ˈθruːpʊt/, open-weight /ˌoʊpən ˈweɪt/
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 05/26
item.description:
Today's framework: Problem → Solution → Impact — Frame the business problem, explain the solution pattern, then close with practical impact.
📰 Stories covered: • Help Net Security / Anthropic: Anthropic adds 28 security and compliance integrations for Claude — Anthropic introduced 28 integrations around the Claude Compliance API so IT and security teams can pipe Claude Enterprise content and activity events into existing DLP, SIEM, identity, eDiscovery, observability, and AI security tools. • Hugging Face Blog: Harness, Scaffold, and the AI Agent Terms Worth Getting Right — Hugging Face published a practical glossary for agent terms such as model, scaffolding, harness, context engineering, policy, tool use, skills, sub-agents, and rollout. • Red Hat Developer: MCP servers vs. skills: Choosing the right context for your AI — Red Hat explained when to use MCP servers versus skills for adding context to large language models, framing MCP as the right path for external services and skills as a way to package repeatable instructions and domain knowledge. • AWS Machine Learning Blog: Agents that transact: Introducing Amazon Bedrock AgentCore payments, built with Coinbase and Stripe — AWS announced Amazon Bedrock AgentCore payments in preview, built with Coinbase and Stripe, so agents can access and pay for web content, APIs, MCP servers, and other agents within an execution loop under spending governance and observability. • Mistral AI News: Emmi joins Mistral to accelerate the AI-native industry — Mistral AI entered a definitive agreement to acquire Physics AI pioneer Emmi AI, adding more than 30 researchers and engineers to its Science and Applied AI teams and targeting real-time engineering simulations and digital twins.
✨ Featured expressions: The common theme in today's stories is that..., That changes the question., Put differently (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: governance //ˈɡʌvərnəns//, harness //ˈhɑːrnəs//, scaffolding //ˈskæfəldɪŋ//
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 05/25
item.description:
Today's framework: PREP — Point -> Reason -> Example -> Point: lead with the takeaway, justify it, ground it in evidence, and restate the action-oriented message.
📰 Stories covered: • Model Context Protocol: The 2026-07-28 MCP Specification Release Candidate — The MCP release candidate removes protocol-level sessions, makes requests stateless, adds first-class extensions including MCP Apps and Tasks, and hardens authorization. • Google Blog: The Gemini app becomes more agentic, delivering proactive, 24/7 help — Google announced Gemini 3. • Mistral AI: Emmi joins Mistral to accelerate the AI-native industry — Mistral entered a definitive agreement to acquire Emmi AI, adding Physics AI talent and models for real-time industrial simulation and digital twins.
✨ Featured expressions: the true potential of AI agents cannot be fully realized or scaled efficiently, moving from a phase where agents were impressive individual feats to a stage where they must operate cohesively, paves the way for a more integrated and secure AI landscape (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: stateless //ˈsteɪtləs//, authorization //ˌɔːθərəˈzeɪʃən//, agentic //eɪˈdʒentɪk//
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 05/24
item.description:
Today's framework: Situation → Complication → Resolution — Set the context, clarify the tension, then land on the practical response.
📰 Stories covered: • Mistral AI: Emmi joins Mistral to accelerate the AI-native industry — Mistral AI entered a definitive agreement to acquire Emmi AI, a Physics AI pioneer for industrial engineering, adding more than 30 researchers and engineers to Mistral Science and Applied AI teams. • Windsurf: Wave 13: Merry Shipmas — Windsurf Wave 13 adds first-class support for parallel, multi-agent sessions, Git worktrees, side-by-side Cascade panes, and a dedicated terminal profile for agent execution. • Modal: Modal's Series C: Raising $355M at a $4.65B valuation — Modal raised $355 million at a $4.65 billion post-money valuation after growing fivefold since September and surpassing $300 million in annualized revenue.
✨ Featured expressions: the center of gravity is moving, not just a talent acquisition, part of the normal software process (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: Physics AI //ˈfɪzɪks ˌeɪ ˈaɪ//, Git worktrees //ɡɪt ˈwɜːrkˌtriːz//, sandboxed execution //ˈsændˌbɑːkst ˌeksɪˈkjuːʃən//
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 05/23
item.description:
Today's framework: What → So What → Now What — Explain what happened, why it matters, and what to watch or do next.
📰 Stories covered: • OpenAI: An OpenAI model has disproved a central conjecture in discrete geometry — OpenAI says a general-purpose reasoning model found a proof that disproves a central conjecture in the planar unit distance problem, first posed by Paul Erdős in 1946. • Cursor: Improvements to Cursor Automations — Cursor brought Automations into the Agents Window and added multi-repo and no-repo automation support, with templates for Slack digests, product analytics, FAQs, finance, and customer health. • Simon Willison: Datasette Agent — Simon Willison announced Datasette Agent, an extensible AI assistant for querying Datasette databases conversationally, generating charts, and running through plugins and local models. • GitHub Changelog: Semantic issue search in Copilot Chat — GitHub made semantic issue search generally available in Copilot Chat on web, letting developers use natural language to find and group issues by intent rather than exact keywords.
✨ Featured expressions: raises the ceiling, a source of original technical insight, cross-system work (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: conjecture //kənˈdʒek.tʃər//, discrete geometry //dɪˈskriːt dʒiˈɑː.mə.tri//, semantic issue search //sɪˈmæn.tɪk ˈɪʃ.uː sɝːtʃ//
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 05/22
item.description:
Today's framework: Situation → Complication → Resolution — Frame the current context, surface the constraint, then show the practical path forward.

🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 04/30
item.description:
Today's framework: What → So What → Now What — Present the facts, unpack the significance, then map out concrete next steps.
📰 Stories covered: • Mistral AI: Remote agents in Vibe + Medium 3.5 — Mistral launched a new mid-tier model and remote coding agents that run entirely in their cloud, no local hardware needed. • Hugging Face: AI evals are the new compute bottleneck — The HAL team spent $40K on agent rollouts; scaffold choice alone creates a 33x cost spread. • Simon Willison: LLM 0.32a0 — Major refactor aligns the popular OSS LLM library with vendor API message formats and typed outputs. • IBM Research: Granite 4.1 — Open Apache 2.0 dense LLMs (3B/8B/30B) with 512K context; the 8B model matches the previous 32B MoE model.
✨ Featured expressions: "What this signals is", "To put that in context", "The bigger picture here" (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: remote coding agents /rɪˈmoʊt ˈkoʊdɪŋ ˈeɪdʒənts/, evaluation scaffold /ɪˌvæljuˈeɪʃən ˈskæfəld/, context window /ˈkɒntekst ˈwɪndoʊ/
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 04/29
item.description:
Today's framework: Before→After→Bridge — Map the friction of yesterday to the opportunity of today, then act.
📰 Stories covered: • OpenAI News: OpenAI models, Codex, and Managed Agents come to AWS — OpenAI and AWS expanded their partnership to bring GPT-5.5, Codex, and Managed Agents natively to Amazon Bedrock in limited preview, letting enterprises access OpenAI's stack within their AWS security perimeter. • Cursor Changelog: Build programmatic agents with the Cursor SDK — Cursor released a TypeScript SDK in public beta giving programmatic access to its agent runtime, enabling CI/CD and automation use cases beyond the IDE. • Hugging Face Blog: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Nano Omni — An open-weights omni-modal model leading benchmarks across documents, GUI, video, and audio with 9.2x throughput gains on video workloads. • Mistral AI: Workflows for work that runs the business — Mistral's Python-based AI orchestration layer enters public preview with durable execution, observability, and human-in-the-loop approvals already in production at ASML and CMA-CGM.
✨ Featured expressions: lower the barrier to entry, within the security perimeter, bridge the gap between X and Y (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: security perimeter /sɪˈkjʊərɪti pəˈrɪmɪtər/, agent runtime /ˈeɪdʒənt ˈrʌntaɪm/, human-in-the-loop /ˈhjuːmən ɪn ðə luːp/
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 04/28
item.description:
Today's framework: Problem→Solution→Impact — Identify the core challenge, present the breakthrough, then quantify the real-world effect.
📰 Stories covered: • OpenAI Engineering: An open-source spec for Codex orchestration: Symphony — OpenAI released Symphony, an open-source spec and agent orchestrator that connects a project-management board (like Linear) to a fleet of continuously running Codex agents. • OpenAI Company: The next phase of the Microsoft OpenAI partnership — OpenAI and Microsoft announced an amended partnership agreement. • OpenAI News: Introducing GPT-5.5 — GPT-5.
✨ Featured expressions: nothing short of transformative, human-in-the-loop model, seismic shift (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: agent orchestrator //ˈeɪdʒənt ˈɔːrkɪstreɪtər//, control plane //kənˈtroʊl pleɪn//, AGI clause //ˌeɪ dʒiː ˈaɪ klɔːz//
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 04/27
item.description:
Today's framework: PREP — Point → Reason → Example → Point. Lead with the takeaway, support it, ground it in examples, then restate the point.
📰 Stories covered: • OpenAI News: Introducing GPT-5.5 — OpenAI launched GPT-5.5 for ChatGPT and Codex, positioning it as a faster and more capable model for coding, computer use, research, and long-horizon work. • Hugging Face Blog: DeepSeek-V4: a million-token context that agents can actually use — Hugging Face analyzed DeepSeek-V4, highlighting 1M-token context models that cut per-token FLOPs and KV-cache requirements enough to make long agent traces more realistic. • OpenAI News: Introducing workspace agents in ChatGPT — OpenAI introduced shared workspace agents for long-running organizational workflows in ChatGPT and Slack.
✨ Featured expressions: To put that in context, Put differently, What this means is (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: orchestration overhead /ˌɔːrkɪˈstreɪʃən ˈoʊvərhed/, long-horizon work /ˌlɔːŋ həˈraɪzən wɝːk/, persistent agent memory /pərˈsɪstənt ˈeɪdʒənt ˈmeməri/
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.

item.title: AI English Daily — 04/26
item.description:
Today's framework: Situation → Complication → Resolution — Set the context, surface the tension or change, then close with the practical resolution.
📰 Stories covered: • Anthropic News: Anthropic and NEC collaborate to build Japan’s largest AI engineering workforce — Anthropic said NEC will deploy Claude across roughly 30,000 employees, build a Japan-based center of excellence, and jointly ship domain-specific AI products for finance, manufacturing, local government, and cybersecurity. • Windsurf Changelog: Wave 13: Merry Shipmas — Windsurf rolled out first-class support for parallel multi-agent sessions, Git worktrees, side-by-side Cascade panes, and made SWE-1.5 Free the default model for all users for the next three months. • Anthropic News: An update on our election safeguards — Anthropic detailed new evaluation methodology, political-bias measurements, public datasets, and external review partnerships meant to keep Claude balanced and accurate on election-related questions.
✨ Featured expressions: "let's dive into today's AI updates", "To put that in context, NEC is establishing a dedicated Japan-based center of excellence", "What this signifies is a concrete signal that AI coding and workflow agents are transitioning from mere pilot mode into large-scale organizational adoption" (and alternatives in show notes) 🔑 Key terms: center of excellence /ˌsɛntər əv ˈɛksələns/, Domain-specific AI products /ˈdoʊmeɪn spɪˈsɪfɪk eɪ aɪ ˈprɒdʌkts/, pilot mode /ˈpaɪlət moʊd/
🎓 Practice tip: try 0.7x-0.9x playback for shadowing.
